Title: Verify Brands Page Load and Structure
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to VIRTO_START Demo Store
Steps:
1. Navigate to https://virtostart-demo-store.govirto.com/brands
2. Wait for page to load completely
3. Verify page structure and elements
Expected Results:
1. Brands page loads successfully without errors
2. Top brands section is displayed with featured brands (Apple, Mall of America, Nordstrom, ZurRose)
3. Alphabetical navigation A-Z is visible and properly organized
4. Search functionality is prominently displayed
5. Page title shows 'Demo - B2B-store - Brands'
6. All UI elements are properly aligned and responsive

Title: Test Brand Search with Full Brand Name
Steps:
1. Locate the search textbox on brands page
2. Type 'Apple' in the search box
3. Click the Search button
4. Observe the results and interface updates
Expected Results:
1. Search executes successfully
2. Only 'Apple' brand is displayed in results
3. Search button text updates to show 'Apple Search'
4. Only section A is displayed, other sections are hidden
5. Alphabetical buttons are properly disabled/enabled based on results
6. Search term remains in the input field

Title: Test Brand Search with Partial Name (Substring)
Steps:
1. Clear any existing search terms
2. Type 'App' in the search box
3. Click the Search button
4. Verify partial matching results
Expected Results:
1. Search finds multiple brands containing 'App'
2. Results show 'Apple' (prefix match) and 'GRAINGER APPROVED' (substring match)
3. Only sections A and G are displayed
4. Alphabetical buttons show only 'All', 'A', and 'G' as active
5. Search is case-insensitive
6. Dynamic interface updates correctly

Title: Test Search Reset Functionality
Preconditions: User has performed a search on brands page
Steps:
1. Ensure there is a search term in the search box
2. Clear the search box (remove all text)
3. Click the Search button
4. Verify reset behavior
Expected Results:
1. Search box is cleared successfully
2. Full brand directory is restored
3. All alphabetical sections A-Z are displayed
4. All alphabetical filter buttons are restored to proper states
5. Complete brand listing is visible
6. URL parameters are properly managed

Title: Verify Alphabetical Navigation System
Preconditions: User is on brands page with full brand listing
Steps:
1. Observe the alphabetical navigation buttons A-Z, 0-9, #
2. Identify which letters are active vs disabled
3. Note the visual indicators for each state
4. Verify the logic of enabled/disabled states
Expected Results:
1. Letters with brands are clickable and properly styled
2. Letters without brands (Q, 0-9, #) are disabled and visually distinct
3. 'All' button is available for full listing
4. Visual distinction between active and inactive states is clear
5. Complete alphabet coverage A-Z is properly organized
6. Navigation is intuitive and user-friendly

Title: Test Individual Brand Page Navigation
Steps:
1. Locate 'Apple' brand in the brand listing
2. Click on the Apple brand link
3. Wait for brand page to load
4. Verify brand page content and functionality
Expected Results:
1. Navigation to Apple brand page is successful
2. URL updates correctly to '/Brands/apple'
3. Page title updates appropriately
4. Brand-specific product catalog is displayed
5. Only Apple products are shown
6. All catalog functionality is available (sorting, filtering, grid/list view)
7. Navigation breadcrumbs are properly maintained

Preconditions: User is on a specific brand page (e.g.
Expected Results:
1. Verify sorting options are available
2. Test grid/list view toggle
3. Check filtering options
4. Verify product display

column11:

Title: Test Brand Page URL and SEO Structure
Preconditions: User has access to multiple brand pages
Steps:
1. Navigate to different brand pages (Apple, Microsoft, Samsung)
2. Verify URL structure for each brand
3. Check page titles and meta information
4. Test direct URL access
Expected Results:
1. URLs follow SEO-friendly format '/Brands/brand-name'
2. Brand names in URLs are properly formatted (lowercase, hyphenated)
3. Page titles reflect the brand name and context
4. Direct URL access works correctly
5. URLs are shareable and bookmarkable
6. No broken links or 404 errors

Title: Verify Brand Directory Completeness
Steps:
1. Navigate through all alphabetical sections
2. Count approximate number of brands
3. Verify brand diversity and quality
4. Check for major brand presence
Expected Results:
1. Brand directory contains 200+ brands
2. Brands span multiple categories (electronics, industrial, consumer goods, food & beverage)
3. Major brands are present (Apple, Microsoft, Samsung, Nintendo, HP, Coca-Cola)
4. Geographic diversity is represented
5. Brand names are properly formatted and displayed
6. No duplicate or broken brand entries

Title: Test Brand Search Performance and Responsiveness
Steps:
1. Perform multiple searches with different terms
2. Measure response time for search results
3. Test rapid search term changes
4. Verify no lag or performance issues
Expected Results:
1. Search results appear instantly without page reload
2. Real-time filtering works smoothly
3. No lag when typing or changing search terms
4. Interface remains responsive during search operations
5. No browser errors or console warnings
6. Search performs well with both short and long search terms

Title: Verify Brand Page Integration with Main Navigation
Preconditions: User is on a brand page
Steps:
1. From a brand page, test main site navigation
2. Use top navigation menu items
3. Test breadcrumb navigation
4. Verify integration with site features
Expected Results:
1. Main navigation remains fully functional on brand pages
2. Breadcrumb navigation shows proper hierarchy
3. Site features (cart, lists, search) work correctly
4. Brand context is maintained where appropriate
5. User can navigate back to main catalog seamlessly
6. No functionality is lost or broken on brand pages

Title: Test Brand Page Mobile Responsiveness
Preconditions: User has mobile device or browser developer tools
Steps:
1. Access brand page on mobile device or simulate mobile view
2. Test search functionality on mobile
3. Verify alphabetical navigation on smaller screens
4. Check touch interactions
Expected Results:
1. Brand page is fully responsive on mobile devices
2. Search functionality works correctly on touch devices
3. Alphabetical navigation adapts to mobile layout
4. Touch interactions are smooth and accurate
5. Text and buttons are appropriately sized for mobile
6. No horizontal scrolling or layout issues
